title: [Bug]: Missing storage.conf options should fallback to default values
body:
### Issue Description

When overwriting e.g. the storage driver in the storage.conf and forgetting setting every other needed option like the graphRoot or runRoot, it can easily happen to overwrite your /root or / with the content of graphRoot etc.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. add a empty storage.conf in /etc/containers
2. Edit storage.conf and only insert 
```
[storage]
driver="btrfs"
```
3. reset your environment with `podman system reset`


### Describe the results you received

`graphRoot: /root`

and all content of `/root` removed (thanks restic for the backup...)

### Describe the results you expected

Storage type switched to btrfs, but all other options stay at default

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Debian bookworm, latest podman version

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
> @computeralex92 [#16795 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16795#issuecomment-1380314681)

that only applies to rootless though

nevermind my last comment.

Yes this is a duplicate of the issue you've reported.  Closing as a dup of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16795
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
